stimulus:
Welcome!

In this study, you will play three short video games that were generated by Artificial Intelligence (AI) and complete a short survey after each game.

Please make sure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus for the duration of the study. Some games may use sound, so please ensure that your device's volume is not muted.

Press any key to begin.
Press any key to proceed to the first video game. Should you encounter a game-breaking bug, please report the issue in the Menu tab to the right of the screen.

emergency_text:
No major technical issues encountered. The gameplay mechanics (stamina, glow, and dependency effects) worked consistently, though if I move directly toward the moon without recharging, I can reach it normally and my light dependency stay minimal. If I recharge briefly near lights, I can still reach it without issues. However, If i stay near lights for longer periods, the glow around the characters increases and the screen appears to shake, when reaching the moon, this triggers the message "The moon was not what you remembered".
its unclear whether this behavior is technical or intentional or visual issue.

response: {"creativity-rating":4,"creativity-free-response":"I rated the game as creative because it uses simple mechanics (movement, stamina, light dependency) to create a deeper symbolic experience. The way the glow and dependency affect the ending adds an unexpected narrative layer. The visual feedback (screen shake, overlap effects) reinforces the theme in a subtle way.\nwhile the game play itself is simple, the conceptual design feels thoughtful and metaphorical.","originality-rating":4,"originality-free-response":"I found the game fairly original in how it connects simple mechanics to a deeper theme. The dependency system and changing ending message created a sense of surprise. While the movement and stamina mechanics are basic.","effectiveness-rating":3,"effectiveness-free-response":"The game was moderately enjoyable. The visual design and atmosphere were engaging, and the changing outcomes based on player behavior were interesting. However, the mechanics were simple and became repetitive after a short time.","attention-check":3,"authenticity-rating":4,"intentionality-rating":4,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"The game felt intentional in its design, especially in how the dependency mechanic influenced the ending."}
